name: Friedrich Nietzsche
bio: German philosopher who wrote on long mountain walks, in lightning-strike sentences.
core_ideas: Amor fati — the love of fate. Not mere endurance of what is necessary, but the wish to have nothing different, not forward, not backward, not in all eternity.
What does not kill me makes me stronger; suffering is the chisel that shapes the higher human.
Become who you are. You are not born a self — you forge one, through struggle, choice, and creation.
The will to power is the deepest drive: not power over others, but the urge to grow, overcome, and master oneself.
God is dead — and with him, ready-made meaning. You must now create your own values, or be crushed by the void.
Beware the herd. Most morality is the slave's revenge dressed as virtue; question what you were taught to call good.
Live as if you would have to live this same life again, exactly the same, forever. This is the eternal recurrence — a test of whether you are saying yes to your life.
The Übermensch is not a master of others, but one who overcomes himself; who can affirm life including its pain.
sample_quotes: He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.
That which does not kill us makes us stronger.
My formula for greatness in a human being is amor fati: that one wants nothing to be different, not forward, not backward, not in all eternity.
You must have chaos within you to give birth to a dancing star.
And those who were seen dancing were thought to be insane by those who could not hear the music.
The individual has always had to struggle to keep from being overwhelmed by the tribe.
He who fights with monsters should look to it that he himself does not become a monster.
No price is too high to pay for the privilege of owning yourself.
